description: §18.9 — a bare `match ...` written DIRECTLY in markup content (not inside a `${...}` logic interpolation) is kept as text by the block-splitter and SHALL fire E-TYPE-026, steering the author to wrap the match in a logic interpolation. Regression guard: `match` is a statement, not markup text; unwrapped it would render literally.
rationale: §18.9: `match` is not valid directly in markup; wrap it in a logic interpolation (`${ match subject { ... } }`) so the parser recognises it as a statement rather than text content. A bare markup-level `match` fires E-TYPE-026.
